name: Trehab
description_short: Trehab Community Action Partnership is a non-profit serving rural Northeastern PA with free community services including food assistance, workforce development, housing, and substance abuse programs.
description_long:
Trehab Community Action Partnership is a non-profit Community Action Agency and Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) operating across seven counties in Northeastern Pennsylvania: Bradford, Sullivan, Susquehanna, Tioga, Wayne, Wyoming, and Pike. The organization was founded to serve as both a service provider and advocate for low-income individuals, the unemployed, underemployed, elderly, and other at-risk groups.

Trehab offers a comprehensive range of free and low-cost community services designed to build economic independence. Their programs include food banking and pantries with community garden produce, workforce development for adults and youth, transportation services via shared-ride programs, housing development and buyer assistance, substance abuse treatment and prevention services, weatherization programs for energy efficiency, and peer-led resource centers. They have created over 100 affordable housing units throughout their years of service and operate emergency food assistance programs.

What distinguishes Trehab is their integrated approach addressing multiple barriers to self-sufficiency simultaneously. Rather than offering isolated services, they function as a comprehensive community action agency with specialized programs for drug and alcohol treatment, Marcellus industry workforce training, and housing revitalization. Their peer-led resource centers and community gardens reflect a strength-based, community-engaged model rather than purely transactional assistance.

Trehab is genuinely positioned as a free-help resource for vulnerable populations in rural areas without commercial financial alternatives. The primary caveat is their geographic limitation to seven specific counties in Northeastern Pennsylvania, making them inaccessible to those outside their service area. Additionally, while their website describes programs, specific eligibility requirements, application processes, and current program capacity are not fully detailed online.
pros: Multi-service non-profit eliminates need to visit multiple agencies for food, housing, employment, and health services
Created over 100 affordable housing units, demonstrating sustained commitment to housing solutions
Peer-led resource centers provide community-based support rather than purely clinical interventions
Community garden program addresses food insecurity with fresh produce in addition to food pantries
Operates as HUD-recognized CHDO with capacity for housing development and homebuyer assistance
Workforce development programs serve multiple populations: adults, youth, and dislocated workers
Weatherization program reduces utility costs, addressing energy burden for low-income households
cons: Service area limited to seven rural Northeastern PA counties; unavailable to most of the country
Website lacks specific details on program eligibility criteria, wait times, and current availability
No online intake or application process described; requires direct contact with physical locations
Substance abuse treatment described minimally; unclear whether services address medication-assisted treatment or counseling-only models
No pricing information or cost-sharing details provided for any programs
best_for: Low-income residents of Susquehanna, Wyoming, Bradford, Sullivan, Tioga, Wayne, or Pike counties seeking multiple services
Job seekers in rural PA needing workforce training and employment support alongside financial assistance
Families experiencing housing instability or seeking affordable homeownership in Northeastern PA
Individuals struggling with substance abuse seeking free outpatient treatment in their community
diagnosis: Trehab is best for low-income individuals in rural Northeastern Pennsylvania counties seeking free, comprehensive community services including food, housing, employment, and substance abuse support. The primary limitation is strict geographic restriction to seven specific PA counties; those outside this region cannot access services regardless of need.
services: Food pantries and food banks with fresh produce via community garden
Workforce development programs for adults, youth, and dislocated workers
Shared-ride transportation for Susquehanna and Wyoming County residents
Affordable housing development and community housing revitalization
Homebuyer assistance and first-time homebuyer programs
Alcohol, tobacco, and drug abuse prevention education
Peer-led resource centers in Wyoming and Susquehanna Counties
Weatherization and home energy efficiency assistance
Community Services for low-income individuals and families
Emergency assistance and community support services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
